mission.title: dex: Adjust scopes to be in line with the Microsoft Identity Platform v2.
mission.description:
#### Overview

This PR adds the openid scope necessary for the Microsoft Identity Platform v2 as well as changes the user and group scopes to the ones now supported on the Microsoft graph API. 
This PR was created together with Microsoft backend engineers and fixes https://github.com/dexidp/dex/issues/1855

#### What this PR does / why we need it

The current Microsoft connector just works. But if you enabled features like 2fa on your Microsoft Azure AD Application this is not enforced, because the scopes used trigger the Azure App in a different way than intended. 

So, to be able to use 2fa on a Microsoft Azure AD App, you need those changes. 

#### Special notes for your reviewer

I tested this change locally with group sync and refresh token. Same behaviour as before, except that policies like 2fa applied to the Azure AD App now are enforced by Microsoft. 

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```release-note
NONE
```

resolution.summary:
#### Overview

Added Microsoft Identity Platform v2 specific scopes in order to also support conditional access policies, like MFA, TermsOfUse, etc. It does not change the behaviour of current implementation and works with or without conditional access policy configured.

We have this fix in production for five months without any issues.

#### What this PR does / why we need it

In order to support enforced policies, like MFA, you need to use the Microsoft Identity Platform v2 with according scopes. The configuration on Dex-side remains the same and works as of today, with or without conditional access, groups and refresh tokens.

See also: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/develop/v2-permissions-and-consent#the-default-scope

Closes #1855

#### Special notes for your reviewer

This PR is based on #2047 and contains the changes to the MS connector only. It has been tested on top of v2.35 and v2.36.

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```release-note
NONE
```
steps: Review the issue discussion for context
Apply the fix from the linked pull request
